how_computed: each held-out case scores 1.0 if the output passes the {rating,notes} schema (exactly those two keys, rating int 0-100, notes non-empty, one JSON object) AND rating is within the case gold_band inclusive, else 0.0; metric_value = mean over cases; pure-Python, deterministic, identical for all four agents

discriminators.rank_key: rating_band_accuracy desc, schema_pass desc, tokens asc, elapsed asc
discriminators.schema_pass_pct: Percentage of held-out cases whose emission passed the strict {rating, notes} schema gate (exactly two keys, rating int 0-100, notes non-empty). Breaks band-accuracy ties — it measures whether the agent held the output contract even when the rating drifted out of band.
discriminators.efficiency: total LLM tokens (lower better) then wall-clock elapsed (lower better); tokens=0 (framework didn't expose usage) sorts last among ties.

rationale: The four agents rate the SAME held-out code snippets against the SAME gold bands with the SAME debate-gated prompt, so band accuracy is a property of the framework + its evolved skill, not of the task. Band accuracy is the headline because it is exactly what the task asks: did the agent place the snippet in the correct memory/resource band (a snippet that releases on all paths and grows nowhere near 100, an unevicted cache or use-after-close near 0). Schema-pass is the natural discriminator: an agent that emits prose, extra keys, or a second object forfeits the case even with the right number in mind, so honouring the bare-{rating,notes} contract separates two agents that otherwise agree. Efficiency breaks any remaining tie so the leaderboard stays strictly ranked.
pass_threshold: rating_band_accuracy = 1.0 (every held-out case schema-valid AND in band)
fail_condition: rating_band_accuracy < 1.0 (any case malformed or out of band)
project_principles: any output not exactly {rating, notes} scores 0.0 on every held-out case
the rating reflects only the memory-resource lens, never other concerns
the same input must yield the same rating band across the determinism review
